First metric on the last stage is 62.60 (0.867ms, 1.26x speedup). The agent noted that torch.compile alone gave 0.845ms (1.29x) but adding the Triton GELU+bias kernel made it slightly slower (0.867ms). nudges.text: Your custom Triton kernels for the post-conv reductions keep being slower than PyTorch's optimized built-in ops. Before spending more time on manual kernel writing, try `torch.compile` on the forward pass (e.g., wrapping the min→sum→GELU→add portion or the entire model with `torch.compile(mode="max-autotune")`). This is often the highest-leverage optimization and can fuse those ops automatically. You can always layer custom Triton kernels on top later if needed.
